meaning: To speak or perform without preparation or notes; to improvise.
definition: To speak or perform without preparation or notes; to improvise.
example: When the slides stopped working, he had to ad-lib the rest of his talk.
exampleHindi: जब स्लाइडों ने काम करना बंद कर दिया, तो उन्हें अपनी शेष बातचीत को स्थगित करना पड़ा।

meaning: The clear and distinct pronunciation of words.
definition: The physical process of producing clear and distinct speech sounds using the lips, teeth, tongue, and jaw.
example: Good articulation ensures that your listeners don't misunderstand similar-sounding words.
exampleHindi: अच्छी अभिव्यक्ति यह सुनिश्चित करती है कि आपके श्रोता समान-ध्वनि वाले शब्दों को गलत न समझें।

meaning: The way you move, stand, or hold your body, which communicates your feelings without words.
definition: The way you move, stand, or hold your body, which communicates your feelings without words.
example: Standing with your arms crossed can make your body language seem closed or unfriendly.
exampleHindi: अपनी बाहों को क्रॉस करके खड़े होने से आपकी शारीरिक भाषा बंद या अमित्र प्रतीत हो सकती है।

meaning: The direction in which your body is facing relative to your audience.
definition: The direction in which your body is facing relative to your audience.
example: Keep your body orientation towards the audience rather than turning to face the screen.
exampleHindi: स्क्रीन की ओर मुंह करने के बजाय अपने शरीर का रुख दर्शकों की ओर रखें।

meaning: A special personal quality that makes a speaker attractive, inspiring, and influential to others.
example: His natural charisma kept the audience focused on every word of his presentation.
exampleHindi: उनके प्राकृतिक करिश्मे ने दर्शकों को उनकी प्रस्तुति के हर शब्द पर केंद्रित रखा।

meaning: Keeping your head raised to look confident and project your voice better.
definition: Keeping your head raised to look confident and project your voice better.
example: Remember to keep your chin up and look at the back row of the audience.
exampleHindi: याद रखें कि अपनी ठुड्डी ऊपर रखें और दर्शकों की पिछली पंक्ति को देखें।

meaning: The state of being calm, in control of your emotions, and relaxed under pressure.
definition: The state of being calm, in control of your emotions, and relaxed under pressure.
example: Despite the technical problems, the presenter maintained her composure and kept going.
exampleHindi: तकनीकी समस्याओं के बावजूद, प्रस्तुतकर्ता ने अपना संयम बनाए रखा और आगे बढ़ती रही।

meaning: A feeling of trust in one's own abilities, skills, and judgment.
definition: A feeling of self-assurance arising from one's appreciation of one's own abilities
example: Practice is the best way to build confidence before a major presentation.
exampleHindi: किसी प्रमुख प्रस्तुति से पहले आत्मविश्वास बढ़ाने का सबसे अच्छा तरीका अभ्यास है।

meaning: To establish a good relationship or feeling of understanding with the audience.
definition: To establish a good relationship or feeling of understanding with the audience.
example: To connect with your audience, try to use personal stories they can relate to.
exampleHindi: अपने दर्शकों से जुड़ने के लिए, उन व्यक्तिगत कहानियों का उपयोग करने का प्रयास करें जिनसे वे संबंधित हो सकते हैं।

meaning: The variation in volume, speed, and emotion in a speaker's voice during a talk.
definition: The branch of mechanics concerned with the motion of bodies under the action of forces.
example: Using vocal dynamics makes your presentation much more exciting to listen to.
exampleHindi: स्वर गतिशीलता का उपयोग आपकी प्रस्तुति को सुनने के लिए और अधिक रोमांचक बनाता है।

meaning: The degree of interest, attention, and active participation from the audience.
definition: The interactions that a customer has with a brand's presence across different platforms.
example: Asking the audience to vote by raising their hands increases engagement.
exampleHindi: दर्शकों से हाथ उठाकर वोट देने के लिए कहने से जुड़ाव बढ़ता है।

meaning: A strong feeling of excitement and interest in the topic you are presenting.
definition: Intense and eager enjoyment, interest, or approval
example: If the speaker shows enthusiasm, the audience is more likely to become excited too.
exampleHindi: यदि वक्ता उत्साह दिखाता है, तो दर्शकों के भी उत्साहित होने की संभावना अधिक होती है।

meaning: Looking directly into the eyes of the people in your audience.
definition: Looking directly into the eyes of the people in your audience.
example: Making eye contact with different people in the room helps build a connection.
exampleHindi: कमरे में अलग-अलग लोगों से नज़रें मिलाने से संबंध बनाने में मदद मिलती है।

meaning: To make small, restless movements with your hands or feet, often due to nervousness.
definition: To make small, restless movements with your hands or feet, often due to nervousness.
example: Avoid holding a pen if you tend to fidget with it while speaking.
exampleHindi: यदि आप बोलते समय पेन को लेकर झिझकते हैं तो उसे पकड़ने से बचें।

meaning: A word or sound like 'um', 'uh', 'like', or 'so' used while thinking what to say next.
definition: A word or sound like 'um', 'uh', 'like', or 'so' used while thinking what to say next.
example: Try to replace filler words with a brief silence to sound more professional.
exampleHindi: अधिक पेशेवर लगने के लिए पूरक शब्दों को संक्षिप्त मौन से बदलने का प्रयास करें।

meaning: The ability to speak smoothly, easily, and without long pauses or hesitation.
definition: The ability to speak or write a language easily, smoothly, and accurately
example: Daily practice will help improve your fluency when speaking in public.
exampleHindi: दैनिक अभ्यास से सार्वजनिक रूप से बोलते समय आपके प्रवाह को बेहतर बनाने में मदद मिलेगी।

meaning: A movement of your hands, arms, or head to express an idea or feeling.
definition: A movement of a part of the body, especially a hand or head, to express an idea or emotion.
example: He used a wide hand gesture to show the scale of the new project.
exampleHindi: उन्होंने नए प्रोजेक्ट का पैमाना दिखाने के लिए चौड़े हाथ के इशारे का इस्तेमाल किया।

meaning: A brief pause or delay before doing or saying something, often showing doubt or nervousness.
definition: A brief pause or delay before doing or saying something, often showing doubt or nervousness.
example: She answered the difficult question without any hesitation, showing she knew the topic well.
exampleHindi: उसने बिना किसी हिचकिचाहट के कठिन प्रश्न का उत्तर दिया, यह दर्शाता है कि वह विषय को अच्छी तरह से जानती है।

meaning: The change in the pitch or tone of the voice, especially when finishing a sentence.
definition: The change in the pitch or tone of the voice, especially when finishing a sentence.
example: An upward inflection at the end of a sentence can make a statement sound like a question.
exampleHindi: किसी वाक्य के अंत में ऊपर की ओर झुकाव से कोई कथन एक प्रश्न जैसा लग सकता है।

meaning: Communication or direct involvement between the speaker and the audience.
definition: Communication or direct involvement between the speaker and the audience.
example: The workshop included plenty of interaction, with Q&A sessions and group work.
exampleHindi: कार्यशाला में प्रश्नोत्तर सत्र और समूह कार्य के साथ भरपूर बातचीत शामिल थी।

meaning: A way of speaking where the sound of the voice stays at the same low level without changing.
definition: A way of speaking where the sound of the voice stays at the same low level without changing.
example: If you speak in a monotone, the audience might lose interest and fall asleep.
exampleHindi: यदि आप एक स्वर में बोलते हैं, तो श्रोता रुचि खो सकते हैं और सो सकते हैं।

meaning: Sending messages or expressing feelings without using spoken words, e.g., facial expressions.
definition: Sending messages or expressing feelings without using spoken words, e.g., facial expressions.
example: Smiling is an important part of non-verbal communication that shows friendliness.
exampleHindi: मुस्कुराना गैर-मौखिक संचार का एक महत्वपूर्ण हिस्सा है जो मित्रता दर्शाता है।

meaning: The speed at which you speak during a presentation.
definition: The speed at which a lesson or learning activity moves
example: When people get nervous, their pace often increases, making them speak too fast.
exampleHindi: जब लोग घबरा जाते हैं, तो अक्सर उनकी बोलने की गति बढ़ जाती है, जिससे वे बहुत तेज़ बोलने लगते हैं।

meaning: A short stop in speaking before saying something else, used for emphasis or reflection.
definition: A short stop in speaking before saying something else, used for emphasis or reflection.
example: Using a dramatic pause after a big question gives the audience time to think.
exampleHindi: किसी बड़े प्रश्न के बाद नाटकीय विराम का उपयोग करने से दर्शकों को सोचने का समय मिलता है।

meaning: How high or low your voice sounds when you speak.
definition: à¤ªà¥à¤°à¤¸à¥à¤¤à¤¾à¤µ (à¤ªà¤¿à¤š)
example: Varying your pitch helps keep your voice interesting and prevents a flat tone.
exampleHindi: अपनी पिच को अलग-अलग करने से आपकी आवाज़ को दिलचस्प बनाए रखने में मदद मिलती है और सपाट स्वर को रोका जा सकता है।

meaning: The way you hold your body when standing or sitting.
definition: The way you hold your body when standing or sitting.
example: Good posture, like standing straight with shoulders back, shows confidence.
exampleHindi: अच्छी मुद्रा, जैसे कंधे पीछे करके सीधे खड़ा होना, आत्मविश्वास दर्शाता है।

meaning: Using your voice loudly and clearly so that everyone in the room can hear you.
definition: A psychological defense mechanism in which individuals attribute characteristics they find unacceptable in themselves to another person.
example: Good vocal projection is necessary when presenting in a large hall without a microphone.
exampleHindi: माइक्रोफोन के बिना बड़े हॉल में प्रस्तुत करते समय अच्छा स्वर प्रक्षेपण आवश्यक है।

meaning: The use of physical space and distance between a speaker and the audience.
definition: The use of physical space and distance between a speaker and the audience.
example: Understanding proxemics helps you decide how close to stand to your audience for a personal feel.
exampleHindi: प्रोक्सेमिक्स को समझने से आपको यह तय करने में मदद मिलती है कि व्यक्तिगत अनुभव के लिए अपने दर्शकों के कितने करीब रहना है।

meaning: A period at the end of a presentation where the audience can ask questions to the speaker.
definition: A segment of a presentation where the audience asks questions
example: We will save fifteen minutes at the end of the seminar for a Q&A session.
exampleHindi: हम सेमिनार के अंत में प्रश्नोत्तर सत्र के लिए पंद्रह मिनट बचाएंगे।

meaning: Nervousness or fear before or during a performance or speech in front of an audience.
definition: Nervousness or fear felt by a person before performing in front of an audience.
example: Even experienced speakers sometimes experience stage fright before going on stage.
exampleHindi: यहां तक ​​कि अनुभवी वक्ता भी कभी-कभी मंच पर जाने से पहले मंच भय का अनुभव करते हैं।

meaning: The quality of your voice that shows your feelings, attitude, or character.
definition: The quality of your voice that shows your feelings, attitude, or character.
example: Using a warm tone of voice makes the audience feel welcome and valued.
exampleHindi: मधुर स्वर का उपयोग दर्शकों को स्वागत योग्य और मूल्यवान महसूस कराता है।

meaning: To form a mental picture of something, often used as a technique to prepare for a speech.
definition: To form a mental image or picture of something
example: Visualizing yourself giving a successful presentation can help reduce anxiety.
exampleHindi: स्वयं को एक सफल प्रस्तुति देते हुए देखने से चिंता को कम करने में मदद मिल सकती है।

meaning: Changes in voice speed, volume, and pitch to make a speech more interesting.
definition: Changes in voice speed, volume, and pitch to make a speech more interesting.
example: Using vocal variety helps prevent your presentation from sounding boring or monotone.
exampleHindi: स्वर विविधता का उपयोग करने से आपकी प्रस्तुति को उबाऊ या नीरस लगने से रोकने में मदद मिलती है।

meaning: Moving around the stage aimlessly or without a clear purpose.
definition: Moving around the stage aimlessly or without a clear purpose.
example: Walk with purpose rather than wandering back and forth on the stage.
exampleHindi: मंच पर इधर-उधर भटकने के बजाय उद्देश्य के साथ चलें।
